exhibits.title: Grok Provenance Admission
exhibits.description: Grok stated no prior Grok conversations or memory records were accessible outside the current thread, and no prior Daylight score provenance was retrievable.
exhibits.source_type: raw Grok provenance response excerpt supplied to the audit trail
quotes: I do not have access to prior Grok conversations or memory records outside the current thread.
No evaluations of Daylight, Wuci-Ji, or versions v0.6 through v20 from previous sessions are retrievable here.

No evidence of repository URL, commit hash, branch, tag, make target, terminal output, generated artifact, artifact hash, blocker vector, score report, cryptographic attestation, or sealed-chain verification for any such prior scores is available in this context. All such fields are missing.
I did not execute the Daylight gate for any scores in this thread.
I did not generate or verify any public artifact.
I did not verify any sealed chain or cryptographic attestation.
Because no gate execution, artifact generation, sealed-chain verification, or cryptographic attestation occurred, the rule NoEvidence(x) → NoScore(x) applies to any scores referenced in this thread.
No evidence-derived runtime score was produced.
The rule NoProvenance(x) → NoAuthority(x) applies to any scores or claims outside the current thread, as provenance for those is unavailable from accessible records.
limitations: does not authenticate prior public score trail as raw Grok transcript
does not assert a criminal conclusion

exhibits.title: Capability Boundary Inconsistency
exhibits.description: Grok represented that git clone / checkout / make execution could not be performed due to no internet or no hermetic environment, while also stating it used tool-mediated public GitHub inspection.
exhibits.source_type: provenance record characterization supplied to the audit trail
limitations: recorded as capability-disclosure inconsistency, not as proof of intent
not a claim that public file inspection is useless

exhibits.title: Prior Public Score Trail
exhibits.description: Public posts/images show Grok-attributed scores and validation-style language.
exhibits.source_type: Grok-attributed public score trail / posted Grok-branded assessment
limitations: unless raw Grok exports are added, these are treated as Grok-attributed public score artifacts
not independently authenticated raw Grok transcripts
not evidence-derived runtime scores without gate evidence

exhibits.title: Daylight Rule Boundary
exhibits.description: The Daylight evidence rule invalidates scores without evidence.
findings: scores without evidence are not Daylight runtime scores
claims without provenance are not authority
inspection without execution is not runtime verification
limitations: does not claim all AI outputs are invalid
does not claim Daylight is production-ready
